purpose: Classifies countries by demographic-geopolitical position to identify differential risk profiles and mispricing
components: Tier 1 - Aging-rich: NATO core members facing defense-vs-entitlement double bind
Tier 2 - Collapsing-middle: Brazil, Turkey, Thailand, Vietnam with highest variance and potential mispricing
Tier 3 - Young-poor: SSA, parts of South Asia facing food security and governance challenges
Cross-tier dynamics: alliance stress, capital flow patterns, security architecture friction
current_assessment: Provides useful taxonomic framework but at 5/10 confidence. Tier 2 mispricing thesis is intriguing but actionability is uncertain. Within-tier heterogeneity is significant.
limitations: Within-tier heterogeneity is significant (e.g., India vs. Indonesia in Tier 3)
Does not account for country-specific institutional and political factors
Tier boundaries are not precisely defined
